title: erigeron delphiniifolius subsp. typicus
intro: Erigeron delphiniifolius subsp. typicus, commonly known as Delphinium-leaved Fleabane, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Erigeron delphiniifolius subsp. typicus include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin conditions, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and minor infections.
